source_observation.boundary: Qiaomu-owned abstraction of public ppt-master axis catalogs. This file stores ids and workflow lessons only; it does not copy upstream templates, prompts, images, or slide designs.

principle: PPT Master quality comes from locking independent axes before page execution: argument mode, deck visual style, image rendering, image palette behavior, and per-image composition type or hero primitive.

design_spec: Visualization Reference List or component_selection rationale
rejected component/template alternatives when a common choice is intentionally skipped
Image Resource List with dimensions, ratio, purpose, layout pattern, acquire_via, status, text_policy, and page_role
presentation_sidecars: notes/total.md or equivalent speaker-notes sidecar for long decks
animations.json or animation_manifest.json when the output is intended to be presentation-ready
svg_output as authored source and svg_final as post-processed preview source for SVG-first routes

learned: Memphis Pop works when vivid geometry is constrained by a fixed page rhythm: anchor chapter pages, dense guide pages, and breathing visual turns.
Festival/event decks need guide components such as KPI cards, stage maps, schedules, ticket tiers, and sponsor/market comparisons, not only poster backgrounds.
High-energy palettes still need an active color budget per page; thumbnails can reveal cropped or over-busy information earlier than full-slide inspection.
ppt169_sugar_rush_memphis.qiaomu_rule: Use Memphis Pop only with explicit color-count limits, short body copy, and stable component plans for schedule/map/ticket pages.

learned: Risograph Zine becomes executable when the constraints are mechanical: duotone palette, 1-3px misregistration, halftone/paper grain, hard edges, and no rounded cards.
Guide decks can stay dense if the page chrome is disciplined: header band, component area, footer/source line, and alternating breathing hero pages.
Source and culture decks benefit from a sources/credits page plus notes that separate visible guide copy from spoken context.
ppt169_indie_bookstore_zine_guide.qiaomu_rule: Treat zine/print styles as production constraints, not decoration; lock forbidden moves such as gradients, soft glass, and rounded cards.

quality_lessons: Mode is the argument skeleton; visual_style is the layout aesthetic. They must be locked separately.
Visual_style does not own HEX values. Palette behavior describes color use; actual colors come from the deck contract.
AI images share one deck-wide rendering and one deck-wide palette behavior. Local image type is per image.
Hero-page images use primitives such as atmospheric or single_subject, not local infographic types.
Source, user, web, formula, or chart assets own evidence. AI backgrounds may only provide atmosphere, context, concept, or texture unless the user explicitly supplies the object.
Image prompt sidecars are part of the design contract: every AI image needs purpose, ratio, composition, safe text area, text policy, and editable foreground boundary before rendering.
Component choices should include selection rationale and rejected alternatives when several common chart/list/guide components could fit.
Speaker notes and object-level animation sidecars are not visual polish; they prove the SVG groups, reading path, and presentation delivery layer were planned separately.
